what_it_does: A single AI-tic registry entry declaring the module-level AI_TIC dict for the 'closure_form' tic: the generic-AI tell of closure-form/summarizing writing, with id, label, a list of marker phrases, and a description.
observations: Module docstring explains the tic as a named Tim frustration (open-future-writing vs closure-form, expansion-ratio<1)
AI_TIC dict has id='closure_form' (matches file stem), label='closure-form'
markers: ['in conclusion','to summarize','in summary','to wrap up','final thoughts',"that's everything"]
desc restates the closure-form definition and 'kills institutional memory' rationale
Docstring references runtime/ai_tics.py + ai_tics/AGENTS.md as the seam/spec
standouts: Markers are concrete closure phrases the fingerprint pass matches — the catalogue data, not a code branch
conventions: Follows the AI_TIC schema mandated by ai_tics/AGENTS.md (id equals file stem, non-empty markers list)
Pure declarative data module with no logic — honors the 'declared DATA, not action' floor
Docstring cross-references the registry and constitution
notes: If briefing someone: this is one catalogue file — a self-registering declaration of the 'closure-form writing' AI tell with six trigger phrases.
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — docstring states it is a seed AI-tic and the file is exactly that declaration.
intention_signals: Tries to encode the closure-form writing pattern as detectable data so the fingerprint pass can subtract it, preserving open-future institutional-memory writing.
novelty: Treating summarizing/closing phrases as a denoisable AI fingerprint to subtract.
contribution: Contributes the concrete marker set and metadata for the closure_form tic in the catalogue.
summary_for_embedding: AI-tic registry file declaring the closure_form AI_TIC dict — id, label, six closure-phrase markers, and a description of closure-form writing as a generic-AI tell.
intention_for_embedding: To catalogue closure-form/summarizing writing as a subtractable generic-AI fingerprint and protect open-future institutional-memory writing.
